lenses.description: A telephoto large-format prime in the Arles set, well suited to portraits, interviews and tighter coverage. It carries the line's clean, modern sharpness and gentle, painterly feel with natural colour and fine skin rendering, a sixteen-blade iris for smooth round bokeh and floating elements holding breathing low. Wide open it softens into a lower-contrast look with shallow, dimensional separation, in the set's consistent housing and gearing.

lenses.description: A telephoto large-format prime in the Arles set, a longer portrait and detail lens with an arrestingly shallow depth of field wide open. It renders with the Arles balance of clean, modern sharpness and a gentle, painterly character, natural colour and a sixteen-blade iris for smooth round bokeh, with floating elements keeping breathing minimal. Opened up it eases into a softer, low-contrast rendering, sharing the set's front and gearing for fast changes.

lenses.description: The widest lens in DZOFilm's flagship Arles Prime set, an ultra-wide large-format prime that opens up sweeping vistas and tight interiors while keeping wide-angle distortion well controlled. It carries the Arles signature: a clean, modern sharpness paired with a gentle, painterly rendering, natural colour and a sixteen-blade iris for round out-of-focus highlights, with floating elements holding breathing to a minimum, and a nano coating keeping flare and ghosting in check. Its front takes no screw-in filter thread, so it works with a matte box rather than screw-in filters.

lenses.description: The longest lens in the Arles set, a telephoto large-format prime for portraits and compressed, isolated coverage. It carries the line's clean, modern sharpness and gentle, painterly rendering with natural colour, a sixteen-blade iris for smooth natural bokeh and floating elements for minimal breathing. Its reach delivers strong subject separation while holding the Arles look, in the set's consistent housing and gearing.

lenses.description: An ultra-wide large-format prime in the Arles set, sitting between the 14mm and 21mm and filling out the wide end of the line. It shares the Arles character: clean, modern sharpness with a gentle, painterly look and natural colour, a sixteen-blade iris for round bokeh, and floating elements that keep breathing minimal. A nano blue coating controls flare and preserves colour fidelity. Its front takes no screw-in filter thread, so it works with a matte box rather than screw-in filters.

lenses.description: The widest lens in DZOFilm's full-frame Gnosis Macro set, a wide-angle prime with true built-in macro capability that lets you get extremely close while still taking in the surrounding scene. It renders with crisp, edge-to-edge sharpness from its close-focus distance out to infinity and a clean, natural colour that stays uniform from in-focus to out-of-focus, with a sixteen-blade iris for smooth, naturally soft bokeh. Because it focuses to infinity as well, it doubles as a wide general-purpose prime, not just a macro.

lenses.description: A wide-standard prime in the full-frame Gnosis Macro set, delivering true life-size built-in macro for cinematic close-ups of small subjects, products and detail. It stays crisp and sharp edge to edge from its close-focus distance through to infinity, with clean, natural colour that carries evenly into the out-of-focus areas and a sixteen-blade iris for soft, natural bokeh. It focuses to infinity too, so it works as a standard prime as well as a macro.

It holds crisp, edge-to-edge sharpness from close focus through to infinity, with clean, natural colour into the out-of-focus areas and a sixteen-blade iris for smooth, soft bokeh. Focusing to infinity, it also works as a short-telephoto portrait prime.

lenses.description: The widest zoom in DZOFilm's Pictor line, a compact, lightweight Super 35 parfocal cine zoom covering the ultra-wide range. Close focusing suits tight spaces, and distortion stays well controlled even wide open. Focus holds through the zoom, internal floating elements keep breathing very low, and a 16-blade iris renders soft, rounded bokeh. Color and handling are matched across the Pictor set, with standard cine gears and an interchangeable PL/EF mount.

It carries the line's modern-vintage balance, sharp and clean yet organic with neutral colour and fine skin rendering, and a sixteen-blade iris for smooth round bokeh with controlled focus breathing. Light and compact for its reach, with the family's shared front diameter and gearing.

sources.introductionYear: CineD 2020: initial 6-lens Vespid Prime kit announced (tier-3, POS-D12)

lenses.description: The widest lens in DZOFilm's original Vespid Prime set, an ultra-wide full-frame cine prime that keeps distortion well controlled for its angle of view. It carries the line's modern-vintage balance: an APO design that stays sharp and clean while rendering with a gentle, organic character and neutral colour, with a sixteen-blade iris for round out-of-focus highlights and minimal focus breathing. Lightweight and compact like the rest of the agile-wasp family, it shares the common gear positions that make swaps quick. Its front carries an anti-reflective extinction thread rather than a filter thread, so it works with a matte box rather than screw-in filters.

Compact and light with the family's uniform front diameter and gear positions for fast changes, plus a detachable rear ND filter.

lenses.description: The close-focus specialist of the original Vespid set, a full-frame macro prime with true built-in macro capability for tight detail and product work. It keeps the line's modern-vintage balance, an APO design that stays sharp and clean while rendering organically with neutral colour, and a sixteen-blade iris for smooth round bokeh with minimal breathing. Relatively small and light for a macro, it shares the family's front diameter and gear positions, making it easy to slot into the set or onto a gimbal.

lenses.description: The wide-standard lens in DZOFilm's Vespid Cyber set, a full-frame prime that inherits the Vespid Prime optical character and adds a built-in data interface. Its APO design renders with the organic, gently sharp Vespid look and a sixteen-blade iris for round bokeh, while an internal-focus design keeps the front from rotating or changing size and holds breathing low. The Cyber data connection outputs focus and aperture data for VFX and virtual-production workflows and enables autofocus with compatible third-party systems, behind a larger front that matches the rest of the Cyber set.

sources.introductionYear: CineD/Newsshooter April 2025: Vespid Prime 2 announced (tier-3, POS-D12)

lenses.description: The longest lens in the second-generation Vespid Prime II set, a full-frame short telephoto with a fast constant T1.9 for portraits and tighter, isolated coverage. Gen 2 retains the Vespid look while upgrading the optics for cleaner, purer imagery with rich detail and natural tonality. Floating elements sharpen close focus, and Cooke /i metadata, a ten-blade iris and a compact all-metal PL body round out a set built for modern, metadata-driven workflows.

lenses.description: The ultra-wide of DZOFilm's second-generation Vespid Prime II set, a compact full-frame prime with an expansive field for landscapes, establishing shots and close-quarters work. Gen 2 keeps the signature Vespid look but upgrades the optics: a fast constant T1.9 across the set, new aspherical elements and nano coatings for cleaner, purer imagery with gentler highlight roll-off and natural skin. Floating elements sharpen close focus, and Cooke /i metadata plus a ten-blade iris and all-metal PL body suit gimbals and modern workflows.

lenses.description: A wide-angle prime in the second-generation Vespid Prime II set, full-frame and fast at a constant T1.9. Gen 2 retains the Vespid character while upgrading to new aspherical elements and nano coatings for cleaner shadows, gentler highlights and natural, controlled contrast. Floating elements aid close focus, and it carries Cooke /i metadata, a ten-blade iris and a compact all-metal PL body that balances well on handheld rigs and gimbals.

lenses.description: The portrait prime of the second-generation Vespid Prime II set, a full-frame short telephoto with a fast constant T1.9 for shallow, controlled separation. Gen 2 keeps the Vespid character but upgrades to new aspherical elements and nano coatings for clean, pure rendering with gentle highlight roll-off and natural skin. Floating elements aid close focus, and Cooke /i metadata, a ten-blade iris and a compact PL body make it easy to balance on gimbals and handheld rigs.

lenses.description: A telephoto prime in the Vespid Retro set, suited to portraits and tighter coverage with a vintage rendering. Built on the original Vespid optics, its golden coating produces a cool, blue-toned image with warm, controllable amber flares and gentle sharpness for a dreamy, art-house feel. A sixteen-blade iris keeps bokeh smooth and round with minimal breathing, in the Retro's silver aluminium housing with the family's shared front and gearing.

lenses.description: The longest lens in the Vespid Retro set, a telephoto full-frame prime for portraits and compressed, isolated coverage with a vintage look. It shares the original Vespid optics and mechanics, while a golden coating gives a cool, blue-toned base with warm, controllable amber flares and a softer, art-house sharpness. A sixteen-blade iris delivers smooth round bokeh with minimal breathing, in the Retro's silver housing.

lenses.description: The widest lens in the Vespid Retro set, an ultra-wide full-frame prime that pairs a controlled wide-angle rendering with the Retro line's vintage character. It shares the original Vespid optics and mechanics, but a golden coating gives a cool, blue-toned base image with warm, controllable amber flares and a gentler sharpness for a sun-soaked, art-house feel. A sixteen-blade iris keeps bokeh round and breathing minimal, all in the Retro's distinctive silver housing. Its front carries an extinction thread rather than a filter thread, so it works with a matte box rather than screw-in filters.

lenses.description: DZOFilm's full-frame X-Tract probe zoom, a specialist macro lens built to reach into narrow, hidden spaces and pull tiny worlds up to cinematic scale. Its slender, spacecraft-styled barrel slips between book pages or into miniature sets, delivering high-magnification macro with crisp, controlled detail and a creamy bokeh that is especially lovely toward the long end. Being a parfocal zoom, it holds focus while you change framing, opening up push-in and vertigo-style moves at macro range, and its rugged, weather-sealed build stands up to demanding close-up and product work.
